segments.text: And he heard the words of Laban’s sons, saying,
Jacob hath taken away all that was our father’s; and of that which was our father’s hath he gotten all this glory.
segments.speaker: Laban’s sons

segments.text: And Jacob beheld the countenance of Laban, and, behold, it was not toward him as before.

footnotes: 31.2 as before: Heb. as yesterday and the day before

segments.text: Return unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred; and I will be with thee.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field unto his flock,
And said unto them,
I see your father’s countenance, that it is not toward me as before; but the God of my father hath been with me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And ye know that with all my power I have served your father.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And your father hath deceived me, and changed my wages ten times; but God suffered him not to hurt me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: If he said thus, The speckled shall be thy wages; then all the cattle bare speckled: and if he said thus, The ringstraked shall be thy hire; then bare all the cattle ringstraked.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Thus God hath taken away the cattle of your father, and given them to me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And it came to pass at the time that the cattle conceived, that I lifted up mine eyes, and saw in a dream, and, behold, the rams which leaped upon the cattle were ringstraked, speckled, and grisled.
Jacob:
segments.speaker: The angel of God

segments.text: Here am I.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Lift up now thine eyes, and see, all the rams which leap upon the cattle are ringstraked, speckled, and grisled: for I have seen all that Laban doeth unto thee.
segments.speaker: The angel of God

segments.text: I am the God of Beth-el, where thou anointedst the pillar, and where thou vowedst a vow unto me: now arise, get thee out from this land, and return unto the land of thy kindred.
segments.speaker: The God of Beth-el

segments.text: And Rachel and Leah answered and said unto him,
Is there yet any portion or inheritance for us in our father’s house?
segments.speaker: Rachel and Leah

segments.text: Are we not counted of him strangers? for he hath sold us, and hath quite devoured also our money.
segments.speaker: Rachel and Leah

segments.text: For all the riches which God hath taken from our father, that is ours, and our children’s: now then, whatsoever God hath said unto thee, do.
segments.speaker: Rachel and Leah

segments.text: ¶ Then Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives upon camels;
And he carried away all his cattle, and all his goods which he had gotten, the cattle of his getting, which he had gotten in Padan-aram, for to go to Isaac his father in the land of Canaan.
And Laban went to shear his sheep: and Rachel had stolen the images that were her father’s.
And Jacob stole away unawares to Laban the Syrian, in that he told him not that he fled.

footnotes: 31.20 unawares…: Heb. the heart of Laban

segments.text: So he fled with all that he had; and he rose up, and passed over the river, and set his face toward the mount Gilead.
And it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob was fled.
And he took his brethren with him, and pursued after him seven days’ journey; and they overtook him in the mount Gilead.
Take heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: 31.24 either…: Heb. from good to bad

segments.text: ¶ Then Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mount: and Laban with his brethren pitched in the mount of Gilead.
What hast thou done, that thou hast stolen away unawares to me, and carried away my daughters, as captives taken with the sword?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Wherefore didst thou flee away secretly, and steal away from me; and didst not tell me, that I might have sent thee away with mirth, and with songs, with tabret, and with harp?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: And hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and my daughters? thou hast now done foolishly in so doing.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: It is in the power of my hand to do you hurt: but the God of your father spake unto me yesternight, saying, Take thou heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: And now, though thou wouldest needs be gone, because thou sore longedst after thy father’s house, yet wherefore hast thou stolen my gods?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Because I was afraid: for I said, Peradventure thou wouldest take by force thy daughters from me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: With whomsoever thou findest thy gods, let him not live: before our brethren discern thou what is thine with me, and take it to thee. For Jacob knew not that Rachel had stolen them.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And Laban went into Jacob’s tent, and into Leah’s tent, and into the two maidservants’ tents; but he found them not. Then went he out of Leah’s tent, and entered into Rachel’s tent.
Now Rachel had taken the images, and put them in the camel’s furniture, and sat upon them. And Laban searched all the tent, but found them not.
Let it not displease my lord that I cannot rise up before thee; for the custom of women is upon me.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: ¶ And Jacob was wroth, and chode with Laban: and Jacob answered and said to Laban,
What is my trespass? what is my sin, that thou hast so hotly pursued after me?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: This twenty years have I been with thee; thy ewes and thy she goats have not cast their young, and the rams of thy flock have I not eaten.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: That which was torn of beasts I brought not unto thee; I bare the loss of it; of my hand didst thou require it, whether stolen by day, or stolen by night.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Thus I was; in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep departed from mine eyes.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Thus have I been twenty years in thy house; I served thee fourteen years for thy two daughters, and six years for thy cattle: and thou hast changed my wages ten times.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Except the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely thou hadst sent me away now empty. God hath seen mine affliction and the labour of my hands, and rebuked thee yesternight.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: These daughters are my daughters, and these children are my children, and these cattle are my cattle, and all that thou seest is mine: and what can I do this day unto these my daughters, or unto their children which they have born?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Now therefore come thou, let us make a covenant, I and thou; and let it be for a witness between me and thee.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: And Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a pillar.
And Jacob said unto his brethren, Gather stones; and they took stones, and made an heap: and they did eat there upon the heap.
And Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed.

footnotes: 31.47 Jegar-sahadutha: that is, The heap of witness, Chaldee; 31.47 Galeed: that is, The heap of witness, Heb.

segments.text: This heap is a witness between me and thee this day. Therefore was the name of it called Galeed;
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: The LORD watch between me and thee, when we are absent one from another.
segments.speaker: Laban

footnotes: 31.49 Mizpah: that is, A beacon, or, watchtower

segments.text: If thou shalt afflict my daughters, or if thou shalt take other wives beside my daughters, no man is with us; see, God is witness betwixt me and thee.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Behold this heap, and behold this pillar, which I have cast betwixt me and thee;
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: This heap be witness, and this pillar be witness, that I will not pass over this heap to thee, and that thou shalt not pass over this heap and this pillar unto me, for harm.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: The God of Abraham, and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge betwixt us.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Then Jacob offered sacrifice upon the mount, and called his brethren to eat bread: and they did eat bread, and tarried all night in the mount.
And early in the morning Laban rose up, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them: and Laban departed, and returned unto his place.
